summary: Large closed, unmerged GX matching pass that continuously integrated PR #441 and was later abandoned for history cleanup/resubmission. The slice shows broad Dolphin GX header/source work, symbol renaming in remaining GX assembly, movement of Dolphin OS/base headers into include/dolphin, removal of old .inc declarations, and unrelated-looking fallout across fighter/statevar files and obj_files.mk. Evidence for final matching impact is limited because the PR was closed unmerged and had no review comments.
what_changed: Added initial C source stubs or implementations for Dolphin GX modules: GX.c, GXInit.c, GXFifo.c, and GXAttr.c.
Added or reorganized GX public/private headers under include/dolphin/gx, including GX.h, GXAttr.h, GXInit.h, GXMisc.h, GXLight.h, gxprivate.h, and edits to gxtypes.h.
Removed legacy GX .inc declaration files such as include/dolphin/gx/GX.inc, GXInit.inc, and GXFifo.inc, indicating a move from assembly include declarations toward C headers.
Renamed many GX assembly references from anonymous labels such as lbl_804D5BA8 to the meaningful global __GXContexts.
Marked some internal GX helper routines as local labels, for example func_8033D1B8 to local_8033D1B8 and func_8033D3E4 to local_8033D3E4 in GX.s.
Inserted decomp.me scratch links and match-progress comments above many GX assembly functions, with progress ranging from 0% to 100% in the excerpt.
Added Dolphin OS/base headers such as OSThread.h, OSInterrupt.h, OSMutex.h, OSContext.h, and PPCArch.h under include/dolphin, while deleting old src/dolphin copies or .inc forms for some headers.
Added large Captain/Falcon C files ftCaptain_SpecialN.c and ftCaptain_SpecialS.c and removed large corresponding assembly files ftfalcon_1.s, ftfalcon_2.s, and ftfalcon_3.s.
Introduced per-character state variable headers for Captain, Fox, GameWatch, Luigi, and Ness, plus src/melee/ft/ftstatevars.h, while touching many fighter special-move files.
Updated obj_files.mk heavily, consistent with adding/removing compiled objects across GX, fighter, and other modules.
The PR was explicitly described as continuously integrating PR #441, then closed with the author comment that they would clean up git history and resubmit.
smart_moves: Replaced opaque small-data symbols with domain names such as __GXContexts, which makes GX context-field reverse engineering and future decomp easier to search and audit.

Separated private GX declarations into gxprivate.h and public declarations into GX*.h headers, matching a reusable Dolphin SDK-style organization instead of keeping declarations in monolithic .inc files.
Kept in-progress GX assembly annotated with decomp.me scratch URLs and percentage notes, preserving the exact matching state for follow-up work even where functions were not yet converted to C.
Demoted apparent file-local helpers from exported func_* labels to local_* labels in assembly, reducing false public API surface and documenting internal call relationships.
Moved OS/base declarations toward include/dolphin paths, which is a cleaner shared-header pattern for code used by multiple Dolphin and game systems.
decomp_lessons: For library decomp passes, meaningful global symbol recovery can be useful even before full C matching; __GXContexts is a concrete example from the excerpt.
When replacing SDK .inc declarations with C headers, introduce public/private boundaries early so later C files can include the correct surface without leaking internal state.
Preserve scratch links and match percentages in large assembly-transition PRs; they give later contributors an entry point and help avoid rediscovering failed attempts.

Be cautious with mega-PRs that mix library matching, header reorganization, build-file churn, and fighter source conversions. This PR was closed for git-history cleanup/resubmission, suggesting scope/history hygiene mattered.
If a PR continuously integrates another branch or PR, document that dependency clearly; otherwise future reviewers may struggle to distinguish original work from integration noise.
Removing old assembly files while adding C sources should be paired with obj_files.mk updates in the same patch to keep the build object list consistent.
naming_conventions: Use Dolphin SDK-style double-underscore globals for known internal library state, as shown by __GXContexts.
Use include/dolphin/gx for public GX headers and gxprivate.h for private GX internals.
Use module-specific GX header names such as GXAttr.h, GXFifo.h, GXInit.h, GXMisc.h, GXLight.h, and gxtypes.h rather than a single catch-all declaration include.
Use local_XXXXXXXX labels for internal assembly helpers when the original func_XXXXXXXX symbol is not intended to be globally exported.
Character state variable headers follow a per-character pattern such as ftFox_StateVars.h, ftNess_StateVars.h, ftLuigi_StateVars.h, ftGameWatch_StateVars.h, and ftCaptain_StateVars.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renamed small-data loads in GX assembly from lbl_804D5BA8@sda21(r13) to __GXContexts@sda21(r13), preserving instructions while improving symbol accuracy.
Annotated assembly functions with decomp.me scratch URLs and measured match percentages, e.g. comments like 3500 (0%), 2270 (33.24%), 260 (89.60%), and 0 (100%).

Converted some globally labelled helper routines to local labels in assembly while updating branch targets to match, e.g. calls to func_8033D1B8 became local_8033D1B8 and calls to func_8033D3E4 became local_8033D3E4.
Kept partially matched GX functions in assembly while adding C files and headers around them, allowing incremental matching without requiring a full module conversion in one step.
Used header declarations to make assembly comments and C source organization converge on known GX/OS interfaces.

review_feedback.text: WIll clean up git history and resubmit.
review_feedback.implication: The PR was closed unmerged for workflow/history cleanup rather than because of documented technical review objections.
The branch was not a clean standalone review unit; future work should isolate integration churn or explain dependencies.

review_feedback.text: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
review_feedback.implication: Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the author’s cleanup/resubmission note.

searchable_terms: clean up git history and resubmit
follow_up_queries: Find the resubmitted PR after #440 that cleaned up git history for gx pass 12.
Compare PR #440 against PR #441 to identify which changes were integration noise.
Search later merged GX PRs for __GXContexts and gxprivate.h to see which conventions survived.
Inspect later Dolphin header cleanup PRs for removal of .inc declarations in favor of include/dolphin headers.
Trace whether ftCaptain_SpecialN.c and ftCaptain_SpecialS.c from this branch were merged in a later fighter PR.
